title: Eggless Brownies
tagline: Fudgy boxed brownies with no eggs — yogurt does the job and nobody can tell.
servingsNote: One standard boxed mix, cut into 9 squares.
macroNotes: Estimated for a typical boxed mix — check your box, they vary. This is a cheat-day treat: sugar and fat heavy, low protein.
One square (~245 kcal) is a reasonable single hit; two starts eating a real chunk of a 1600–1800 kcal day.

Protein boost that actually works in brownies: swap the plain yogurt for Greek yogurt, or whisk a scoop of chocolate whey into the batter (add a splash more liquid if it stiffens). That's the right move here since soya/savoury protein doesn't belong in a dessert.

items: 1/2 cup (120 g) plain yogurt — replaces both eggs (1/4 cup per egg)
Oil and water as stated on the box
Optional: chocolate chips or chopped nuts

steps.title: Preheat
steps.body: Heat the oven to the temperature on the box (usually ~180°C / 350°F) and grease or line your baking tin.

steps.title: Replace the eggs
steps.body: Instead of 2 eggs, use 1/2 cup (120 g) plain yogurt — 1/4 cup per egg. Yogurt is the best swap here: it keeps the brownies moist and fudgy and adds no competing flavour (unlike banana). Applesauce (1/4 cup per egg) also works if you prefer.

steps.title: Mix the batter
steps.body: Combine the brownie mix with the 1/2 cup yogurt plus the oil and water quantities printed on the box. Stir until just combined — don't overmix. Fold in chocolate chips or nuts if using.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Pour into the tin and bake for the box's stated time (roughly 25–35 min). Since yogurt makes a slightly denser batter than eggs, check a few minutes early — a skewer should come out with a few moist crumbs, not wet batter.

steps.title: Cool and cut
steps.body: Let the brownies cool fully in the tin before cutting into 9 squares — they firm up as they cool and cut much cleaner.

notes: Other egg swaps that work per egg: 1/4 cup mashed banana (adds banana flavour), or 1 tbsp vinegar + 1 tsp baking soda (leavening, best combined with one of the wet swaps). Yogurt is the cleanest all-rounder.
Eggless batter is a touch denser, so slightly under-baking keeps them fudgy rather than cakey.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
